title: Bolbitis nigra
intro: Bolbitis nigra, also known as the black maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Lomariopsidaceae, characterized by its dark green, leathery fronds. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Bolbitis nigra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Bolbitis nigra is often used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Bolbitis nigra is classified as a species of fern, specifically within the genus Bolbitis and family Dryopteridaceae. This classification is based on its characteristics and morphological features. The genus Bolbitis is a group of ferns that are mostly native to tropical and subtropical regions. Within the family Dryopteridaceae, Bolbitis nigra is one of several hundred species of ferns. The classification of Bolbitis nigra continues to be refined as new research and data become available.

side_effects: Bolbitis nigra is typically used as an ornamental plant in aquariums, but like many aquatic plants, its potential side effects are not well-documented.
